old_claim.note: Municipal Water Supply provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No municipal-water dependency remains because analogous underground distribution does not make a water-supply system causally necessary for manufactured-gas street lighting.

source_shape.source_locator: The Met documents coal-gas lighting at Pall Mall in 1807 and does not identify municipal water supply as part of the lighting system.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The historical installation is explained through manufactured coal gas, public lamps, and the Pall Mall deployment.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Shared pipe-network form is an analogy, not a prerequisite; industrial gas production preserves the actual supplied medium and system mechanism.

ontology_before: Municipal gas lighting depended on the neighboring but technically separate municipal water-supply network.
ontology_after: Piped gas lighting depends on gas production and supply without asserting that a water utility enabled it.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: municipal_water_supply is no longer a direct prerequisite for municipal_gas_lighting.
would_reject_if: A source showed the first scoped gas network directly reused an operating municipal water-supply system as required infrastructure.
The node were broadened to a generic municipal utility-network concept rather than gas lighting.
short_reason: A similar pipe network is not a causal water-system dependency.
